channel.title: UnixPulse
channel.description: Notes on Linux, VMware & Web Dev

item.title: VMware vSphere VM Tagging Guide: How to Organize Virtual Machines and Extend Tags into Aria Operations
item.description: Learn how vSphere tagging works, why it matters in large environments, and how to extend VM tags into VMware Aria Operations for dynamic monitoring groups.
item.content:encoded:
If you manage more than a few dozen virtual machines in VMware vSphere, you will quickly realize that organizing workloads becomes difficult. Folders and naming conventions alone are rarely enough to manage large environments efficiently.

Read more →

item.title: AIX Filesystem Management: How to Check and Resize Mount Points
item.description: A practical guide to managing filesystems in IBM AIX. Learn how to check filesystem size, increase or reduce mount points, and understand LVM commands like chfs, lsvg, and lslv.
item.content:encoded:
Sharing my notes on AIX filesystem management from 12 years of experience. This is how I usually handle checking and resizing mount points using commands like df, chfs, lsvg, and lslv.

Read more →

item.title: Oracle Linux 8: Reset Failed Login Attempts Using Faillock
item.description: How to unlock a user account after failed login attempts in Oracle Linux 8 using the faillock command. Quick guide to check and reset the failed login counter.
item.content:encoded:
Here's a quick guide on how to reset the failed login attempts counter for a user in Oracle Linux using the faillock tool.

Read more →

item.title: Oracle Linux 8: How to Reset Root Password
item.description: Step-by-step guide to reset a forgotten root password in Oracle Linux 8 using single user mode and GRUB bootloader. Includes SELinux relabeling steps.
item.content:encoded:
How to reset the root password in Oracle Linux 8 by entering single user mode via the GRUB bootloader. Includes explanations for mount, chroot, and SELinux relabeling.

Read more →

item.title: AIX Filesystem Management: How to Check and Resize Mount Points
item.description: A practical guide to managing filesystems in IBM AIX. Learn how to check filesystem size, increase or reduce mount points, and understand LVM commands like chfs, lsvg, and lslv.
item.content:encoded:
Sharing my notes on AIX filesystem management from 12 years of experience. This is how I usually handle checking and resizing mount points using commands like df, chfs, lsvg, and lslv.

Read more →

item.title: VMware Aria Operations 8.10: Node Primary Replica Failed to Go Offline
item.description: How to fix the Aria Operations cluster when the Primary Replica node fails to go offline and cluster status is stuck on 'Going Offline'. Step-by-step CLI solution.
item.content:encoded:
Part 3 of 3: After fixing filesystem issues, the cluster was stuck in "Going Offline" state. Here's how to force the cluster offline via CLI using vrops-status and sed commands.

Read more →

item.title: VMware Aria Operations 8.10: Failed to Start File System Check on /dev/disk/by-label/root
item.description: How to fix the 'Failed to Start File System Check on /dev/disk/by-label/root' error in VMware Aria Operations 8.10. Manual filesystem check on standard filesystems.
item.content:encoded:
Part 2 of 3: After fixing the LVM filesystem issue, the Data node failed to boot with a root filesystem error. Here's how to run e2fsck on standard filesystems.

Read more →

item.title: VMware Aria Operations 8.10: Failed to Start File System Check on /dev/data/db
item.description: How to fix the 'Failed to Start File System Check on /dev/data/db' error in VMware Aria Operations 8.10. Step-by-step guide to run manual filesystem check on LVM filesystems.
item.content:encoded:
Part 1 of 3: After a storage vMotion, Aria Operations stopped collecting data. The Primary Replica failed to boot with an LVM filesystem error. Here's how to fix it.

Read more →

item.title: Steps to Upgrade vRealize Network Insight from 6.9.0 to 6.14.1 Using Single-Click Offline Upgrade
item.description: Step-by-step guide to upgrade vRealize Network Insight (vRNI) from version 6.9.0 to 6.14.1 using the single-click offline upgrade method. Includes upgrade path, pre-requisites, and verification steps.
item.content:encoded:
I had a sudden request from a customer to upgrade their vRealize Network Insight deployment from version 6.9.0 to 6.14.1. Here's the complete walkthrough using the Single-Click Offline Upgrade method, including the required upgrade path through versions 6.11.0 and 6.13.0.

Read more →

item.title: How to Update VMware vCenter Using the CD-ROM (Offline) Method
item.description: Step-by-step guide to update VMware vCenter Server using the offline CD-ROM method. Download the ISO, upload to datastore, and update without internet access to Broadcom's repository.
item.content:encoded:
If your vCenter Server doesn't have internet access to Broadcom's update repository—or you simply prefer to have more control over the update process—the CD-ROM method is your best option. Here's how to do it step by step.

Read more →

item.title: Introducing Bloom: A Beauty & Wellness HTML Template
item.description: Meet Bloom — a modern, lightweight HTML template for beauty salons, spas, and wellness centers. Pure vanilla HTML, CSS, and JavaScript with dark mode, full accessibility, and zero dependencies.
item.content:encoded:
I've been working on something new: Bloom — a modern, lightweight HTML template designed specifically for beauty salons, spas, wellness centers, skincare brands, and hair studios. Built with pure vanilla HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. No frameworks, no dependencies.

Read more →

item.title: How to Update VMware vCenter 8.0 Update 3b to 3g via URL
item.description: Step-by-step guide to update VMware vCenter Server from 8.0 Update 3b to 8.0 Update 3g using the VAMI URL method. Includes pre-requisites, backup steps, and the full update process.
item.content:encoded:
Keeping vCenter Server updated is one of those tasks that sounds simple but can go sideways if you skip the prep work. This guide walks through updating vCenter Server from 8.0 Update 3b to 8.0 Update 3g using the URL method in VAMI.

Read more →

item.title: How to Use Broadcom Token to Update vCenter Server via VAMI
item.description: vCenter VAMI can't connect to Broadcom's update repository? Here's how to configure the VAMI update URL with your Broadcom token to download vCenter updates.
item.content:encoded:
I was planning to update my vCenter to 8.0 Update 3g and while running through my pre-check activities, noticed that the VAMI couldn't connect to Broadcom's update repository. Here's how to fix it using your Broadcom token.

Read more →

item.title: How to Use Broadcom Token to Download ESXi Updates in vCenter Lifecycle Manager
item.description: Can't download ESXi updates from Broadcom's repository? Here's how to generate a token and configure vCenter Lifecycle Manager to connect to the new Broadcom depot URLs.
item.content:encoded:
I ran into an issue recently while trying to update my ESXi hosts using vCenter Lifecycle Manager—the latest releases weren't showing up. Here's how to fix it using your Broadcom token.

Read more →

item.title: ESXi Host Disconnected: 6 Quick Checks Before Calling Support
item.description: Step-by-step troubleshooting guide for ESXi host disconnection issues in vCenter. Learn the difference between Not Responding and Disconnected states, and fix common problems fast.
item.content:encoded:
There's nothing quite like the sinking feeling you get when you open vSphere Client and see one or more of your ESXi hosts showing as grey and unresponsive. Whether it says "Not Responding" or "Disconnected," your first instinct might be to panic and immediately call VMware support.

But here's the thing—most ESXi disconnection issues can be resolved in minutes with some methodical troubleshooting. I've dealt with dozens of these situations, and the majority turn out to be simple problems with quick fixes.

Read more →

item.title: Making Persistent Nginx Changes in VMware Chargeback Docker Containers
item.description: Learn why your nginx config changes keep disappearing in VMware Chargeback and how to make them stick using Docker templates. A practical guide for PCI compliance and configuration persistence.
item.content:encoded:
Yesterday I ran into one of those frustrating issues that makes you question your sanity. I was working on PCI compliance requirements for VMware Chargeback 8.10, and needed to modify the nginx configuration. Simple enough, right? I made my changes to /etc/nginx/nginx.conf, tested everything, confirmed it worked perfectly, and called it a day.

Then I rebooted the appliance. All my changes were gone. Completely wiped. It was like I'd never touched the file.

Read more →

item.title: Fixing the 'Collecting but Not Receiving Data' Warning in vROps NSX ALB Adapter
item.description: How to fix the 'Collecting but Not Receiving Data' warning in VMware vRealize Operations NSX ALB adapter when connection tests pass but data isn't flowing.
item.content:encoded:
Ran into an odd one recently with VMware vRealize Operations 8.10 and the NSX Advanced Load Balancer adapter. The adapter was stuck in a Warning state. Here's what happened and how I fixed it.

Read more →

item.title: Getting Started with Linux: A Beginner's Journey
item.description: My notes on starting out with Linux, from choosing a distro to setting up your first server.
item.content:encoded:
My notes on starting out with Linux, from choosing a distro to setting up your first server.

Read more →

item.title: Deploy a Static Website with GitHub + Cloudflare
item.description: Step-by-step guide to hosting your website for free using GitHub Pages and Cloudflare.
item.content:encoded:
Step-by-step guide to hosting your website for free using GitHub Pages and Cloudflare.

Read more →

item.title: VMware ESXi Basics: What I Wish I Knew Earlier
item.description: A practical introduction to VMware ESXi for those just starting with virtualization.
item.content:encoded:
A practical introduction to VMware ESXi for those just starting with virtualization.

Read more →
